images.caption: Figure 1:Overview ofSceneCode.Given a natural language scene prompt, our framework compiles it into an executable, code-driven indoor scene with interactable objects.
Figure 2:Overview ofSceneCode.From room-level planning to code-driven object generation, simulation-ready compilation, and scene-state registration.
Figure 3:Room-level qualitative comparison.SceneCode shows better prompt fidelity than the baselines. See AppendixI.1for additional examples.

Figure 4:Object-level qualitative comparison.(a) Mesh and UV layouts of representative assets from SceneCode versus SAM 3D Objects. (b) Code-level editability: locally re-executing one object program with different parameters yields variant objects. (c) On-demand articulated objects with prescribed structure or material that retrieval-based pipelines cannot satisfy.

Figure 5:Robot interaction with generated articulated assets.A SceneCode-generated articulated object is imported into MuJoCo for contact-based robot manipulation. The movable parts produced by the Articulated Object Program remain independent links with compiled joints, allowing the robot to physically open or slide them.

header: Appendix BRoute-Specific Prompt Design for Code-Driven Object Generation

images.caption: Figure 6:Route-specific prompt ablation.Generic prompting can produce visually plausible assets, but route-specific construction prompts better preserve semantic parts, internal structure, material grounding, and articulation-ready components.

header: Appendix ESceneCode Assets versus Prior Asset Strategies

images.caption: Figure 7:User-study interface.Screenshot of the UI used in our user study.

Figure 8:Additional room-level demonstrations.SceneCodegenerates kitchen, basement, dining room, living room, bathroom, and bedroom scenes with prompt-faithful object coverage, spatial layout, and stylistic attributes.

Figure 9:Additional object-level demonstrations.Rendered objects and corresponding wireframes for furniture, ceiling objects, wall objects, and manipulands generated bySceneCode.
The examples show that code-generated assets can express materials, numbers, text, and nontrivial geometric structure while preserving explicit mesh organization.

Figure 10:Articulated object demonstrations.SceneCodegenerates articulated household objects with correct geometric structure, storage interiors such as shelves and partitions, and movable components that remain available for downstream interaction.
